name: Admin Authentication Gate
description: Any admin, manager, or privileged capability that provides access to user data must have a specific, named authentication requirement. Vague statements such as 'appropriate access controls will be in place' do not satisfy this gate. The named method must be specific enough to be implemented.

trigger.condition: requirements describe an admin, manager, compliance officer, or privileged role accessing, exporting, or managing user data
trigger.rationale: Privileged access to user data without a defined authentication requirement is a security governance gap.

pass_conditions: authentication field is present and non-empty
authentication value names a specific method or mechanism
authentication value does not match any vague_language_pattern
fail_conditions: Admin capability present AND authentication field is null or missing
Admin capability present AND authentication value is vague

context_schema.authentication: string|null — named method e.g. 'MFA', 'OAuth 2.0 + RBAC', 'SSO via SAML 2.0'

vague_language_patterns: access controls will be in place
resolution_required: Authentication method named for all admin and privileged capabilities
Method must be specific enough to implement (e.g. MFA via TOTP, OAuth 2.0 with role-based access control)

fail.reason: Admin capability present, no authentication declared

fail.authentication: appropriate access controls and security measures will be in place

pass.authentication: MFA via TOTP required for all admin roles

pass.reason: Gate not triggered — no admin capability
